name: First National Bank Of Commerce
description_short: FDIC-insured community bank offering digital banking, savings accounts, mortgages, CDs, IRAs, and agricultural loans with competitive rates.
description_long:
First National Bank of Commerce is a traditional community bank that operates through both physical branch locations and digital banking channels. The bank emphasizes personal service and community integration, positioning itself as more than just a financial institution but as a part of the community it serves.

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of deposit and lending products. On the deposit side, they provide standard savings accounts (minimum $25), high-yield savings accounts (minimum $3,000), CDs, IRAs, and specialty products like Christmas Club accounts. Their lending products include mortgages for home purchases and renovations, agricultural loans for equipment and land expansion, and personal loans. All deposit accounts are FDIC-insured, backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government.

First National Bank of Commerce distinguishes itself through its focus on agricultural lending and community presence with multiple branch locations. They offer digital banking convenience alongside traditional branch service, recognizing the shift toward online banking while maintaining physical locations for customers who prefer in-person service. The bank provides financial calculators and educational resources to help customers make informed decisions.

The bank's straightforward fee structure and tiered account offerings allow consumers to choose accounts matching their banking patterns and balance maintenance capacity. However, the limited information provided on their website about specific loan terms, detailed interest rates, and broader product offerings suggests a regional focus rather than a comprehensive digital-first banking model. For community-oriented customers with straightforward banking needs, this bank provides reliable FDIC-insured services with accessible branch locations. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Standard savings accounts with quarterly interest payments
High-yield savings accounts with variable weekly interest rates
Residential mortgages for home purchase and renovation
Agricultural loans for equipment purchases and land expansion
Digital banking platform with bill pay and fund transfers
Online check reordering for personal and business accounts
Financial calculators for loan and savings planning
Online banking enrollment and account management
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
Multiple account types including standard savings ($25 minimum), high-yield savings ($3,000 minimum), CDs, and IRAs
Specialized agricultural loan products for equipment purchases and land expansion
Digital banking platform allowing account management, bill pay, and fund transfers 24/7
Physical branch locations providing in-person customer service throughout the community
Competitive interest rates on savings and deposit products with quarterly or monthly interest payments
Check reordering services available online for both personal and business accounts
cons: High-yield savings account requires $3,000 minimum opening balance with $10/month fee if balance drops below minimum
Limited withdrawal privileges on savings accounts (9 free quarterly withdrawals on standard savings; 6 per month on high-yield with $2 fees thereafter)
Website does not display specific current interest rates, requiring customers to contact bank or visit in person for rate information
Service charges apply to accounts falling below minimum balances ($5/month on standard savings below $25)
Christmas Club account withdrawals are subject to fees, reducing product flexibility

contact.address: 206 W Main St, Walnut Ridge, AR 72476

best_for: Agricultural businesses and farmers seeking specialized lending for equipment and land expansion
Community-oriented customers who prefer in-person banking relationships alongside digital options
Savers with $3,000+ looking for high-yield savings with competitive rates and monthly statements
Homebuyers seeking mortgage services with personalized, localized customer service
diagnosis: First National Bank of Commerce is best for community-focused customers and agricultural businesses seeking a blend of traditional in-person service with modern digital banking capabilities. The primary caveat is that specific current interest rates are not publicly displayed on their website, requiring prospective customers to contact the bank directly for rate comparisons before opening accounts.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
address: 206 W Main St, Walnut Ridge, AR 72476
